name: Scroll Progress Driver
summary: The runnable home for the Scroll Progress intent (#1452) — a continuous 0..1 driver sourced from scroll position. Drives two effects native-first: a reading-progress bar (reference: scroll) and a parallax reveal card (reference: view, range: cover). Where CSS Scroll-driven Animations are supported the work is compositor-driven with zero JS (animation-timeline: scroll()/view() + animation-range); otherwise an @supports fallback drives the same progress from a scroll handler / IntersectionObserver. prefers-reduced-motion resolves both to a static end-state (the progressbar still reports position). Backlog #1452, ratified #1407.
description:
Concept

A live surface for the Scroll Progress intent — the continuous 0..1 driver that realizes animation-orchestration's trigger: on-scroll. Two consumers of the same driver: a page-level reading-progress bar (reference: scroll, axis: block) and an element-relative parallax reveal (reference: view, range: cover).

Native-first lowering

Behind an @supports (animation-timeline: scroll()) guard the effects bind animation-timeline: scroll(root block) / view(block) with animation-range: cover — main-thread-free, zero JS. The fallback drives the identical progress via a passive scroll handler + IntersectionObserver. The page reports which lowering is active.

Composition — Motion

Under prefers-reduced-motion: reduce both effects resolve to their static end-state: the parallax never translates, and the progress bar still reports scroll position (a status, not motion).
